rationale_summary: Post-Focus-NFe-cutover, post-Sevilha-Monday-launch batch. Theme: harden the closing loop (reconciliation gaps, audit trail, multi-firm scaling) and pre-empt the next compliance cliff (SPED EFD-Reinf v2.1.2, eSocial S-1010, Reforma Tributária CBS/IBS dual-run). Mix of compounding platform primitives (event sourcing, multi-tenant isolation hardening) and Mini-autonomous slices (column-level audit, idempotency keys, NFSe coverage gaps).

candidates.title: Reforma Tributaria CBS/IBS dual-run shadow ledger
candidates.subtitle: 2026-01 mandatory dual-run of old + new tax regime — needs shadow calc engine before Q4
research_questions: What is the exact 2026-01 dual-run scope (PIS/COFINS+ICMS+ISS vs CBS+IBS) per LC 214/2025?
Which Receita Federal endpoints/SPED layouts ship CBS/IBS reporting and on what timeline?
Can shadow calculations reuse the existing tax_prefs_audit chain or do we need a parallel ledger?
candidates.rationale: Reforma Tributária dual-run starts 2026-01-01 and every Brazilian SaaS accounting product must compute both regimes in parallel. Sevilha's analysts will be the canary: if Contably can't show side-by-side CBS/IBS vs legacy by Q4 2026, every customer churns to a competitor that can. This is the single highest-leverage compliance bet of the next 12 months and compounds into pricing, reporting, and audit features.

candidates.title: NFSe nacional padrao DPS migration coverage
candidates.subtitle: 2026 NFSe unified standard rolls per-municipality — Sevilha's clients span 4+ cities
research_questions: Which Sevilha-client municipalities have already migrated to the NFSe nacional DPS layout vs legacy ABRASF?
Does Focus NFe cover the new DPS endpoints or do we need a fallback provider per city?
What's the manifestação/cancellation flow under the new standard vs the city-by-city quirks today?
candidates.rationale: NFSe is the single largest source of analyst pain — every city has its own layout, and the federal unified DPS standard is rolling out asymmetrically through 2026. Sevilha already serves multiple municipalities; if Contably misses one city's cutover, the analyst manually re-keys invoices. Focus NFe migration just landed (#465–#482) so the moment to widen coverage is now while the abstraction is fresh.

candidates.title: Column-level audit trail (event-sourced who-changed-what)
candidates.subtitle: Mini-autonomous: extend tax_prefs_audit pattern to all financial tables for SPED traceability
research_questions: Which tables (invoices, journal_entries, reconciliations) need column-level audit for Receita Federal traceability?
Can we generalize the tax_prefs_audit trigger pattern from migration 061 to a generic audit_log generator?
What's the storage cost projection at 100 firms × 10k entries/month × 5 years retention?
candidates.rationale: Receita Federal Article 1.179 requires 5-year traceability of every financial mutation. Today only tax_prefs has audit (PR #501). Generalizing this is high-leverage compounding — unlocks SPED audits, customer-facing change-log UI, and dispute resolution — and is Mini-autonomous-feasible because the pattern is already live in one table. Single-file generator + per-table migration.

candidates.title: Idempotency keys on Pluggy + Focus NFe webhook receivers
candidates.subtitle: Mini-autonomous: HMAC-verified webhooks need replay protection — currently silent dupes
research_questions: What idempotency-key headers do Pluggy and Focus NFe send today (Idempotency-Key, X-Request-Id, event UUID)?
Where do webhook duplicates currently get silently absorbed vs cause double-entry bugs?
Should the dedup window be event-id-based (forever) or time-windowed (24h)?
candidates.rationale: Bug #110 (chave vs chave_nfe field mismatch) showed how silent webhook drops cascade into reconciliation gaps. Replays from Pluggy retries and Focus NFe retransmits already happen daily. Adding a webhook_idempotency_keys table + decorator is a single-file Mini job that prevents an entire class of double-counting bugs before Sevilha hits volume.

candidates.title: EFD-Reinf v2.1.2 + eSocial S-1010 catalog sync
candidates.subtitle: Layout v2.1.2 mandatory 2026-07; rubricas catalog drifts monthly — needs scheduled sync
research_questions: What's the diff between EFD-Reinf v2.1.1 (current) and v2.1.2 (2026-07 mandatory) layouts?
How is the eSocial S-1010 rubricas catalog versioned and what's the official sync endpoint?
Can we reuse the Focus NFe daily-reconciliation cron pattern (#473) for catalog sync?
candidates.rationale: EFD-Reinf v2.1.2 is the next deadline after Reforma Tributária prep. Sevilha doesn't process payroll yet but the second customer almost certainly will. The S-1010 catalog mismatch is the #1 cause of eSocial rejections industry-wide — solving it as scheduled sync (not on-demand fetch) is a moat.

candidates.title: Multi-tenant query isolation invariant tests
candidates.subtitle: Mini-autonomous: pytest harness that fails on any query missing company_id filter
research_questions: How does PR #494 (Pluggy TanStack cache scoping bug) generalize — what other caches/queries leak across tenants?
Can SQLAlchemy events intercept all queries to assert company_id is in WHERE clause?
What's the test-time perf cost of running every endpoint test under a 2-tenant fixture?
candidates.rationale: PR #494 was a real cross-tenant leak that shipped to staging. As Contably scales from Sevilha (1 firm) to many, the blast radius of one missed filter is catastrophic (LGPD fines + customer trust). A SQLAlchemy listener + invariant fixture is single-file Mini work that turns this into a class-of-bugs-prevented forever.

candidates.title: Reconciliation gap radar — daily delta surface
candidates.subtitle: NF-e/Pluggy/SPED triangle drift detector — analyst dashboard before month-close
research_questions: What are the canonical 3-way reconciliation rules: NF-e issued vs bank settled vs SPED-classified?
Where do gaps first surface today — Copiloto weekly summary, Minha Agenda, or only at close?
Can we surface deltas in the Copiloto coaching panel (#475) instead of a new screen?
candidates.rationale: Sevilha's analyst pain is concentrated at month-close when she discovers NF-e issued without bank confirmation, or bank entries without invoices. Every accounting SaaS competitor has this radar; Contably ships closing flow without it. Compounds with Copiloto (already built, #495) and Monthly Closing pre-flight gate (#477).

candidates.title: Compliance debt registry — deadline calendar primitive
candidates.subtitle: Compliance_debt: machine-readable registry of every fiscal deadline + impact + owner
research_questions: Which fiscal deadlines does Sevilha currently track manually (SPED, DCTF, EFD-Reinf, NFSe per city)?
Is there a canonical Receita/SEFAZ feed for deadline changes or do we mirror manually?
Should this be a Contably-internal primitive (drives Copiloto alerts) or a customer-facing feature?
candidates.rationale: Today fiscal deadlines live in @TODO comments and PR titles (#465 Focus NFe 2026-07-31, Reforma 2026-01-01). Without a registry, every deadline becomes a fire drill. As a primitive this powers Copiloto warnings, Minha Agenda, marketing comparison pages, and engineering planning. Tagged compliance_debt because today the debt is invisible.

rejected_below_threshold.reason: score < 1.5 — pure platform UX, no Sevilha pain, no compounding
score < 1.5 — t0-2 already covers 5.3→5.6; further upgrade is premature

rejected_below_threshold.theme: Switch from Pluggy to Belvo as primary OFX
rejected_below_threshold.reason: cost >> pain — Pluggy works for Sevilha; provider swap is huge cost with no current customer pain
Sevilha-relevance 2; pre-PMF, white-glove onboarding is correct; auto-cut at this stage
engineering_cost 1, score < 1.0 — analysts work on desktop; mobile is a 2027 conversation

rejected_below_threshold.theme: AI-generated journal entries from invoice description
rejected_below_threshold.reason: duplicate of Copiloto roadmap (T1-1 family); already in flight, not research-worthy
